channel.title: IEEE Spectrum
channel.description: The latest technology news and analysis from IEEE Spectrum

item.title: New silicon photonics chip shows promise for analog AI accelerators
item.dc:creator: Olivia Hsu
Kalhan Koul
item.description:
A research collaboration between universities and industry has demonstrated a silicon photonics chip that performs analog vector-matrix multiplications at low power, showing promise for next-generation AI accelerators. The team published their results this week in a peer-reviewed journal, claiming throughput numbers competitive with commercial digital ASICs at a fraction of the energy cost.

The chip integrates microring resonators in a wavelength-multiplexed configuration, allowing parallel multiply-accumulate operations across multiple wavelengths simultaneously. The researchers say the approach scales naturally with channel count, though calibration overhead remains a concern for production deployment.

Industry observers note that several startups are already targeting similar architectures, and the academic result helps establish performance baselines. The team's preprint is available on arXiv pending the peer-reviewed publication.
